evidence.rule_summary_from_author: LogList is an object table with Log column 0x03, Serial column 0x04, and HighSecurity column 0x05. The Log column references the associated Log table and SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host. The Serial column is the circular-log cursor and SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host. Because Log and LogList are kept in sync, there SHALL be no ACL to allow a Log table to be deleted via the Table table; the LogList object's Delete method must be used instead.

source_snippets.text: 5.8.2.2 LogList (Object Table) The LogList table is an object table that contains exactly one row for each Log table, and contains information about that log. The LogList row with UID=0x00 0x00 0x0A 0x02 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x01 is automatically created on SP issuance with the name Log. A corresponding Log table is also created at Issuance. The uid of the created Log table is referenced in the LogList’s Log column. At creation, the initial LogList table row SHALL have default values of HighSecurity=false and a Serial column value that is a uidref to the UID column value of the first row in the Log table to be used. Table 234 LogList Table Description | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|--------------|----------|-------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | Name | Yes | name | | 0x02 | CommonName | Yes | name | | 0x03 | Log | | Table_object_ref | | 0x04 | Serial | | log_row_ref | | 0x05 | HighSecurity | | boolean |

5.8.2.2.4 Log This is the unique identifier of the associated Log table. This is the same as the unique identifier recorded in the Table table entry for that log table. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

5.8.2.2.5 Serial This is the cursor for the associated Log table. The log is circular. The Serial column value is the UID column value of the most recently added entry in the Log table, and is updated for each log entry, in the order defined by the Next column values in the Log table. Any row of the Log table that has LogKind = 0 marks that row as free and unused. At Log table creation, before any log entries have been added, the Serial column value SHALL be the null uid. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

5.8.4.4 Deleting a Log Table Because a Log table and the LogList table is kept in sync, there SHALL be no ACL to allow the Log table to be deleted via the Table table. The LogList object’s Delete method SHALL be used to delete a Log table. Successful invocation of that method deletes the Log table and its associated entries in both the LogList table and the Table table. For more information, see the Log Template Life Cycle section 5.8.5.

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity
